summary: Frontend-only regression and performance-focused validation completed for 3.3.0 changes (Ramadan tab removal from nav/settings controls, changelog/version updates, and bundle/lazy-load stability).
action_items: Optional: add data-testid attributes for BottomNav links and Settings changelog open controls to improve e2e reliability.
Optional: address Vite warning about /src/lib/db.ts being both static and dynamic imported if deeper chunk isolation is desired.
Optional: resolve Radix Dialog accessibility warning (missing Description/aria-describedby) shown in console.
critical_code_review_comments: SettingsPage.tsx remains very large (>2200 lines), which increases long-term regression risk and maintainability overhead.
Bottom navigation and several critical Settings actions still lack data-testid coverage, weakening robust automation.
Current mixed static+dynamic db.ts imports reduce the effective benefit of deferred loading in some paths.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Validated on Chromium + production build: app loads, Ramadan tab is removed from BottomNav, Ramadan visibility controls are absent in Settings, Settings shows v3.3.0, post-update changelog popup appears when wise-last-seen-version is set to older value (verified in Arabic UI), /ramadan route remains directly accessible, and no blocking runtime errors were found.
rca of the issue: No blocking regression reproduced. Initial popup-flow false negative was test-timing related (splash screen delay and localized title matching), not product logic failure.
